prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French  
Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n’importe quel argument.

Japanese  
わたしたちの間の沈黙は、どんな議論よりも多くを語っていた。

Arabic  
الصمتُ بيننا كان يقول أكثرَ ممّا كان يستطيع أيُّ جدالٍ أن يقوله.

(Tricky phrase)  
Preserving the idea that the silence itself “speaks” more forcefully than an argument is delicate in Japanese and Arabic. In Japanese I chose 語っていた (“was speaking/relating”) rather than 言った (“said”) to give the silence an active voice, and in Arabic I used كان يقول to keep the imperfect nuance of “was saying/used to say,” emphasizing the ongoing eloquence of the silence.
